zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Vaux於1334年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾諾廷加姆時，她的父親John Vaux14歲，母親Mrs. Sibyl Vaux12歲。她和Simon Leeke至少有6個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1390年在英國諾廷加姆爾可坦享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Vaux于1334年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾諾廷加姆时，她的父亲John Vaux14岁，母亲Mrs. Sibyl Vaux12岁。她和Simon Leeke至少有6个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1390年在英国諾廷加姆爾可坦享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Vaux was born in 1334, in Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, John Vaux, was 14 and her mother, Mrs. Sibyl Vaux, was 12. She had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with Simon Leeke. She died in 1390, in Cotham, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Vaux est née en 1334, à Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Vaux, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Sibyl Vaux, avait 12 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille avec Simon Leeke. Elle est décédée en 1390, à Cotham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Vaux 1334 in Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Vaux 14 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Sibyl Vaux 12. Sie hatte mit Simon Leeke mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1390 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Cotham, Nottinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Vaux è nata il nel 1334 nel Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, England, sua padre, John Vaux, aveva 14 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Sibyl Vaux, aveva 12 anni. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia con Simon Leeke. Morì nel 1390, nel Cotham, Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Vaux(이)가 1334년 영국 노틴그함실르 노틴그함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Vaux(은)는 14세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Sibyl Vaux(은)는 12세였다. 그녀는 Simon Leeke(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1390년 영국 노틴그함실르 컷함에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Vaux nasceu em 1334, em Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Vaux, tinha 14 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Sibyl Vaux, tinha 12 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha com Simon Leeke. Ela faleceu em 1390, em Cotham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Vaux родилась в 1334 г. в местности ноттингхам, ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Vaux было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Sibyl Vaux, 12. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь от Simon Leeke. Она умерла в 1390 г. в местности котам, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Vaux nació en 1334, en Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Vaux, tenía 14 años y su mamá, Mrs. Sibyl Vaux, tenía 12 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija con Simon Leeke. Murió en 1390, en Cotham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años.
